name: Llucena - Water Mills Route - Lloma Bernat Loop
name_original: El Alto Maestrat — Circular desde Llucena del Cid hasta el Molí del Ros
description: This difficult 23.8-mile mountain biking route from Llucena del Cid explores the Alt Maestrat, featuring diverse terrain and historic water
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Lucena Del Cid are set within a rugged, mountainous landscape in the province of Castellón, characterized by significant altitude differences and deep ravines. The region, situated at 568 meters above sea level, offers varied terrain suitable for challenging mountain biking. Proximity to the Penyagolosa massif provides routes through pine forests and rocky areas, while the Lucena River carves out features like natural pools. This diverse topography creates an ideal setting for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Lucena Del Cid The most popular mountain bike trail is Llucena - Water Mills Route - Lloma Bernat Loop, a 23.8 miles (38.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 57 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the area's historic watermills and natural river features. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Views of Lucena – Venta Molló loop from Llucena, a difficult 37.4 miles (60.2 km) path. This route offers extensive views of Lucena Del Cid and the surrounding mountainous terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Atzeneta del Maestrat – Mas de la Parra loop from Figueroles, a 29.1 miles (46.8 km) trail leading through rugged terrain with significant elevation changes, often completed in about 4 hours 35 minutes. Mountain biking around Lucena Del Cid is defined by mountainous terrain, deep ravines, and proximity to the Penyagolosa massif. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to challenging climbs. The routes in Lucena Del Cid are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. Nearly 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Lucena Del Cid's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Lucena Del Cid?
A: There are over 110 mountain bike trails around Lucena Del Cid, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. The region's diverse terrain, from deep ravines to mountainous paths, provides a rich environment for exploration.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Lucena Del Cid?
A: The mountain bike trails in Lucena Del Cid cater to various skill levels. You'll find approximately 7 easy routes, around 57 moderate routes, and about 48 difficult routes, ensuring options for both casual riders and experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Lucena Del Cid?
A: Lucena Del Cid experiences mild winters and cool summers, making it suitable for mountain biking year-round. The pleasant climate allows for comfortable riding in most seasons, though checking local weather conditions before your ride is always recommended.

Q: Are there any loop mountain bike trails in Lucena Del Cid?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Lucena Del Cid are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931955479/llucena-water-mills-route-lloma-bernat-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llucena - Water Mills Route - Lloma Bernat Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38293672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Lucena – Venta Molló loop from Llucena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Lucena Del Cid?
A: While many routes are challenging, there are approximately 7 easy mountain bike trails available in Lucena Del Cid. These routes are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, providing a good starting point for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while mountain biking in Lucena Del Cid?
A: Mountain biking in Lucena Del Cid offers stunning natural scenery. You'll encounter rugged mountainous terrain, deep ravines, and lush pine forests, especially near the Penyagolosa massif. The Lucena River also carves out beautiful features like natural pools and historic watermills.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions accessible by mountain bike near Lucena Del Cid?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can explore routes that pass by historic watermills, such as those on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e931955479/llucena-water-mills-route-lloma-bernat-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llucena - Water Mills Route - Lloma Bernat Loop&lt;/a&gt;. Additionally, nearby settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Xodos&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atzeneta del Maestrat&lt;/a&gt;, along with various castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3161495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalatén Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offer cultural points of interest.

Q: Can I find routes with viewpoints or panoramic vistas?
A: Absolutely. The mountainous terrain around Lucena Del Cid provides numerous opportunities for breathtaking views. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38293672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Lucena – Venta Molló loop from Llucena&lt;/a&gt; offer extensive panoramas of the town and surrounding landscapes. The proximity to the Penyagolosa massif also means routes with potential views towards its summit, the 'ceiling' of the Valencian Community.

Q: Are there any routes that feature waterfalls or natural pools?
A: Yes, the Lucena River area is known for its water features. The 'Ruta dels Molins d'Aigua' (Watermill Route) guides you past natural springs and extraordinary natural pools perfect for a refreshing stop. You might also encounter features like Toll de Salt with its beautiful waterfall, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5526642" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carbo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument in the vicinity.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Lucena Del Cid?
A: The mountain biking experience in Lucena Del Cid is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders often praise the challenging climbs, the diverse and rugged natural landscape, and the extensive network of trails that showcase the region's raw beauty and provide a true adventure.

Q: Are there any particularly long-distance mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a longer challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14858928" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;María Cristina Reservoir loop from Figueroles&lt;/a&gt; cover approximately 77 kilometers (48 miles). These routes often feature significant elevation changes and require good endurance.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The terrain is predominantly mountainous and rugged, characterized by significant altitude differences. You'll encounter winding roads, singletracks through pine forests, rocky sections, and paths along deep ravines. Some areas, like Mas de la Costa, feature very steep ramps, with gradients up to 22%.
tips: Also known as the Hermitage of Sant Joan Baptista, it is a site that is not well known but still has its charm. Located in a very unique environment, it also allows you to have very nice views of the entire Alto Maestrat.

A unique site with a beautiful waterfall and natural water pool, very refreshing for summer days. It is located on the riverbed of the Llucena River, which gives it a very pleasant and picturesque environment.

faqs.question: How challenging is the mountain biking on this route?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult mountain bike route, requiring a very good level of fitness. It features significant ascents and varied terrain, including stony paths where you might need to push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied, ranging from natural paths and forest sections to stretches along riverbeds. Expect some wider tracks, but also stony paths and potentially rocky downhill sections, typical of mountain biking in the Castellón province.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in natural beauty and historical elements. You'll pass by crystal-clear water pools, small waterfalls, and several ancient mills, including the historically significant Molí del Ros. You'll also get panoramic views of the valley and the village of Lucena del Cid, and pass near the Atzeneta del Maestrat Castle.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The circular route starts from Llucena del Cid. While specific parking details aren't provided, you can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the village, which serves as the trailhead for this loop.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is particularly appealing during warmer months, as there are opportunities to cool off in natural river pools. However, always check local weather conditions before heading out, especially for mountain biking in mountainous regions.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many trails in the region allow dogs, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially given the varied terrain and potential for wildlife encounters. Ensure your dog is fit enough for a difficult, long mountain bike ride.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required to access this mountain bike route. However, always respect private property and local regulations.

faqs.question: Are there opportunities for swimming or cooling off along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route traverses landscapes featuring crystal-clear water pools and small waterfalls, offering excellent opportunities to cool off, especially during warmer weather. These natural river pools are a highlight of the ride.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The Alt Maestrat region is known for its diverse biodiversity. Riders might observe various flora and fauna, including raptors, wild boars, foxes, and badgers, particularly in the forested sections.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You will encounter parts of the Camí Natural Ruta dels Molins d'Aigua, El Camí IT6TR1 El Camí Central: de Castelló de la Plana a Montserrat, and Llucena - Sant Joan de Penyagolosa.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the difficult rating and varied terrain, you should bring appropriate mountain biking gear, plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit, and navigation tools. Sun protection is also advisable, along with swimwear if you plan to enjoy the natural pools.
